---
title: "STATS 170 - Final Project"
author: "AJ Fong - 006513485"
date: "2026-06-07"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(astsa)
library(xts)
library(dplyr)
library(vars)

# SOURCE: https://www.stlouisfed.org/research/economists/mccracken/fred-databases
```

## PREPROCESSING

```{r}
#===============================================
### PREPROCESSING FRED DATA ### 
#===============================================
# 1. Read raw data
fred <- read.csv("fred_data.csv")

# 2. Subset columns
fred_data <- fred[, c("sasdate", "T10YFFM", "S.P.500", "CPIAUCSL", "UNRATE")]

# Unemployment Rate produced by Chicago Fed for October 2025
fred_data$UNRATE[802] <- 4.4
# Source: https://www.reuters.com/world/us/us-unemployment-rate-rounds-up-44-october-chicago-fed-estimates-2025-11-06/

# Calculate based off of the increase from September 2025 - November 2025 and 
# adjusted for inflation
fred_data$CPIAUCSL[802] <- 323.8

# 3. Define transformation function
transform_fred <- function(x, code) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  
  if (code == 1) {
    # Level (no transformation)
    return(x)
    
  } else if (code == 2) {
    # First difference
    return(c(NA, diff(x)))
    
  } else if (code == 3) {
    # Second difference
    return(c(NA, NA, diff(x, differences = 2)))
    
  } else if (code == 4) {
    # Log level
    return(log(x))
    
  } else if (code == 5) {
    # First difference of log
    return(c(NA, diff(log(x))))
    
  } else if (code == 6) {
    # Second difference of log
    return(c(NA, NA, diff(log(x), differences = 2)))
    
  } else if (code == 7) {
    # Difference of growth rate
    return(c(NA, NA, diff(diff(log(x)))))
    
  } else {
    warning(paste("Unknown transformation code:", code))
    return(x)
  }
}

# 4. Save dates and transformation codes from row 1
dates <- fred_data[-1, 1]
codes <- as.numeric(fred_data[1, -1])

# 5. Remove the transformation-code row from data matrix
fred_data <- fred_data[-1, ]

# 6. Apply transformations to every variable
fred_transformed <- as.data.frame(
  Map(
    transform_fred, 
    fred_data[-1], 
    codes
  )
)

# 7. Restore names and bind dates
names(fred_transformed) <- names(fred_data)[-1]

fred_transformed <- cbind(
  DATE = as.Date(dates, format = "%m/%d/%Y"), 
  fred_transformed
)

# 8. Remove NA rows
fred_transformed <- na.omit(fred_transformed)

# 9. Select data after October 1, 2020
fred_transformed <- fred_transformed[fred_transformed$DATE > as.Date("2020-10-01"), ]
```

## EDA

```{r}
#================================
### EDA ###
#================================
layout(matrix(c(1, 2, 1, 3), 2, 2))
tsplot(fred_transformed$T10YFFM, main = "10-Year Treasury Constant 
       Maturity Minus Federal Funds Rate Differenced", ylab = "Value")
acf(fred_transformed$T10YFFM)
pacf(fred_transformed$T10YFFM)
```

```{r}
layout(matrix(c(1, 2, 1, 3), 2, 2))
tsplot(diff(fred_transformed$T10YFFM), main = "10-Year Treasury Constant 
       Maturity Minus Federal Funds Rate Differenced Twice", ylab = "Value")
acf(diff(fred_transformed$T10YFFM))
pacf(diff(fred_transformed$T10YFFM))
```


```{r}
layout(matrix(c(1, 2, 1, 3), 2, 2))
tsplot(fred_transformed$S.P.500, main = "S&P 500 Price Differnced of Log", ylab = "Price")
acf(fred_transformed$S.P.500)
pacf(fred_transformed$S.P.500)
```

```{r}
layout(matrix(c(1, 2, 1, 3), 2, 2))
tsplot(fred_transformed$CPIAUCSL, main = "CPI for All Urban Consumers: All 
       Items (Seasonally Adjusted) Differenced Twice", ylab = "Value")
acf(fred_transformed$CPIAUCSL)
pacf(fred_transformed$CPIAUCSL)
```

```{r}
m1 <- lm(fred_transformed$CPIAUCSL ~ time(fred_transformed$DATE))
plot(scale(m1$residuals), main = "Scaled Resdiual Plot of CPI", ylab = "Standard Deviation", lwd = 2)
```


```{r}
layout(matrix(c(1, 2, 1, 3), 2, 2))
tsplot(fred_transformed$UNRATE, main = "Unemployment Rate Differenced", ylab = "%")
acf(fred_transformed$UNRATE)
pacf(fred_transformed$UNRATE)
```

```{r}
layout(matrix(c(1, 2, 1, 3), 2, 2))
tsplot(diff(fred_transformed$UNRATE), main = "Unemployment Rate Differenced Twice", ylab = "%")
acf(diff(fred_transformed$UNRATE))
pacf(diff(fred_transformed$UNRATE))
```

```{r}
#=========================================
### TRANSFORMING VARIABLES ###
#=========================================
fred_transformed$T10YFFM <- c(NA, diff(fred_transformed$T10YFFM))
fred_transformed$UNRATE <- c(NA, diff(fred_transformed$UNRATE))

fred_transformed <- na.omit(fred_transformed)

# Convert the cleaned data frame into an xts object
fred_xts <- xts(
  x = fred_transformed[, -1], 
  order.by = fred_transformed$DATE
)
head(fred_xts)
```


## MODEL SELECTION

```{r}
# Restructuring Order for VAR: Real Economy - Prices - Policy - Markets
var_input <- fred_transformed[, c("UNRATE", "CPIAUCSL", "T10YFFM", "S.P.500")]

# Determine optimal lag length
lag_selection <- VARselect(var_input, lag.max = 6, type = "const")

# View the recommended lags
print(lag_selection$selection)

# Estimate the model
var_model <- VAR(var_input, p = 2, type = "const")

# View coefficients, R-squared, and p-values for each equation
summary(var_model)

# Test for serial correlation up to 12 months (Annual cycle)
bg_test <- serial.test(var_model, lags.bg = 12, type = "BG")
print(bg_test)
```

```{r}
# Estimate the model
var_model2 <- VAR(var_input, p = 1, type = "const")

# View coefficients, R-squared, and p-values for each equation
summary(var_model2)

# Test for serial correlation up to 12 months (Annual cycle)
bg_test <- serial.test(var_model2, lags.bg = 12, type = "BG")
print(bg_test)
```


```{r}
# Test if T10YFFM and S.P.500 jointly "Granger-cause" UNRATE
granger_unrate <- causality(var_model2, cause = c("T10YFFM", "S.P.500"))
print(granger_unrate)
```

```{r}
# Test if T10YFFM and S.P.500 jointly "Granger-cause" UNRATE
granger_unrate <- causality(var_model, cause = c("T10YFFM", "S.P.500"))
print(granger_unrate)
```


## IRF SETUP

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# 1. The Monetary Policy Shock (FEDFUNDS)
# ==============================================================================
# Simulating an unexpected 1-percentage-point hike by the Fed
irf_fed_shock <- irf(
  var_model, 
  impulse = "T10YFFM", 
  response = c("UNRATE", "CPIAUCSL", "S.P.500"), 
  n.ahead = 12, 
  ortho = TRUE, 
  boot = TRUE,      # Runs bootstrapping for confidence intervals
  runs = 500,       # Number of bootstrap replications
  ci = 0.95         # 95% confidence intervals
)

plot(irf_fed_shock, main = "Shock from T10YFFM")

# ==============================================================================
# 2. The Financial Market Shock (S.P.500)
# ==============================================================================
# Simulating an unexpected upward jump in stock returns
irf_sp_shock <- irf(
  var_model, 
  impulse = "S.P.500", 
  response = c("UNRATE", "CPIAUCSL", "T10YFFM"), 
  n.ahead = 12, 
  ortho = TRUE, 
  boot = TRUE, 
  runs = 500, 
  ci = 0.95
)

plot(irf_sp_shock, main = "Shock from S.P.500")
```


## FORECASTING

```{r}
# Forecast based off of the VAR(2) model
var_forecast <- predict(var_model, n.ahead = 12)
# ==============================================================================
# VARIABLE 1: UNRATE (Twice-Differenced Inversion & Plotting)
# ==============================================================================
# Clean historical data
clean_unrate <- na.omit(as.numeric(fred_data$UNRATE))
history_unrate_ts <- ts(clean_unrate, start = c(1959, 1), frequency = 12)

# 1. Get historical anchors needed to invert two rounds of differencing
last_unrate       <- tail(clean_unrate, 1)      
last_diff1_unrate <- tail(diff(clean_unrate), 1)

# 2. Reconstruct the first-difference forecasts
fc_unrate_diff1       <- last_diff1_unrate + cumsum(var_forecast$fcst$UNRATE[, "fcst"])
fc_unrate_diff1_lower <- last_diff1_unrate + cumsum(var_forecast$fcst$UNRATE[, "lower"])
fc_unrate_diff1_upper <- last_diff1_unrate + cumsum(var_forecast$fcst$UNRATE[, "upper"])

# 3. Reconstruct the raw percentage level forecasts
actual_unrate_fcst  <- last_unrate + cumsum(fc_unrate_diff1)
actual_unrate_lower <- last_unrate + cumsum(fc_unrate_diff1_lower)
actual_unrate_upper <- last_unrate + cumsum(fc_unrate_diff1_upper)

# 4. Synchronize time indices and attach last historical point to the forecast
unrate_end <- time(history_unrate_ts)[length(history_unrate_ts)]
unrate_fcst_ts  <- ts(c(last_unrate, actual_unrate_fcst),  start = unrate_end, frequency = 12)
unrate_lower_ts <- ts(c(last_unrate, actual_unrate_lower), start = unrate_end, frequency = 12)
unrate_upper_ts <- ts(c(last_unrate, actual_unrate_upper), start = unrate_end, frequency = 12)

# 5. Generate the Plot
plot(history_unrate_ts, type = "l", col = "black", lwd = 1.5, xlim = c(2022.2, 2027.2),
     ylim = c(3, 5),
     main = "Unemployment Rate (%)", xlab = "Year", ylab = "%")
lines(unrate_fcst_ts, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
lines(unrate_lower_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)
lines(unrate_upper_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)

# ==============================================================================
# VARIABLE 2: T10YFFM (Twice-Differenced Inversion & Plotting)
# ==============================================================================
# 1. Clean historical data
clean_fed <- na.omit(as.numeric(fred_data$T10YFFM))
history_fed_ts <- ts(clean_fed, start = c(1959, 1), frequency = 12)

# Get historical anchors needed to invert two rounds of differencing
last_fed       <- tail(clean_fed, 1)
last_diff1_fed <- tail(diff(clean_fed), 1)

# 2. Reconstruct the first-difference forecasts
fc_fed_diff1       <- last_diff1_fed + cumsum(var_forecast$fcst$T10YFFM[, "fcst"])
fc_fed_diff1_lower <- last_diff1_fed + cumsum(var_forecast$fcst$T10YFFM[, "lower"])
fc_fed_diff1_upper <- last_diff1_fed + cumsum(var_forecast$fcst$T10YFFM[, "upper"])

# 3. Reconstruct the raw percentage level forecasts
actual_fed_fcst  <- last_fed + cumsum(fc_fed_diff1)
actual_fed_lower <- last_fed + cumsum(fc_fed_diff1_lower)
actual_fed_upper <- last_fed + cumsum(fc_fed_diff1_upper)

# 4. Synchronize time indices
fed_end <- time(history_fed_ts)[length(history_fed_ts)]
fed_fcst_ts  <- ts(c(last_fed, actual_fed_fcst),  start = fed_end, frequency = 12)
fed_lower_ts <- ts(c(last_fed, actual_fed_lower), start = fed_end, frequency = 12)
fed_upper_ts <- ts(c(last_fed, actual_fed_upper), start = fed_end, frequency = 12)

# 5. Plot Federal Funds Rate Spread
plot(history_fed_ts, type = "l", col = "black", lwd = 1.5, xlim = c(2022.2, 2027.2),
     ylim = c(-2.5, 2.5),
     main = "10-Year Treasury Constant \nMaturity Minus Federal Funds Rate", 
     xlab = "Year", ylab = "%")
lines(fed_fcst_ts, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
lines(fed_lower_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)
lines(fed_upper_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)
# ==============================================================================
# VARIABLE 3: CPIAUCSL (Second Log-Differenced Inversion & Plotting)
# ==============================================================================
# 1. Clean historical data
clean_cpi <- na.omit(as.numeric(fred_data$CPIAUCSL))
history_cpi_ts <- ts(clean_cpi, start = c(1959, 1), frequency = 12)

# Get the two historical anchors needed for log-inversion
last_cpi_level   <- tail(clean_cpi, 1)           
last_log_cpi     <- log(last_cpi_level)          
last_log_diff1   <- tail(diff(log(clean_cpi)), 1)

# 2. Reconstruct the first log-difference forecasts
fc_cpi_logdiff1       <- last_log_diff1 + cumsum(var_forecast$fcst$CPIAUCSL[, "fcst"])
fc_cpi_logdiff1_lower <- last_log_diff1 + cumsum(var_forecast$fcst$CPIAUCSL[, "lower"])
fc_cpi_logdiff1_upper <- last_log_diff1 + cumsum(var_forecast$fcst$CPIAUCSL[, "upper"])

# 3. Reconstruct the log level forecasts 
fc_cpi_log       <- last_log_cpi + cumsum(fc_cpi_logdiff1)
fc_cpi_log_lower <- last_log_cpi + cumsum(fc_cpi_logdiff1_lower)
fc_cpi_log_upper <- last_log_cpi + cumsum(fc_cpi_logdiff1_upper)

# 4. Exponentiate log levels back to the raw index scale
actual_cpi_fcst  <- exp(fc_cpi_log)
actual_cpi_lower <- exp(fc_cpi_log_lower)
actual_cpi_upper <- exp(fc_cpi_log_upper)

# 5. Synchronize time indices
cpi_end <- time(history_cpi_ts)[length(history_cpi_ts)]
cpi_fcst_ts  <- ts(c(last_cpi_level, actual_cpi_fcst),  start = cpi_end, frequency = 12)
cpi_lower_ts <- ts(c(last_cpi_level, actual_cpi_lower), start = cpi_end, frequency = 12)
cpi_upper_ts <- ts(c(last_cpi_level, actual_cpi_upper), start = cpi_end, frequency = 12)

# 6. Plot Consumer Price Index
plot(history_cpi_ts, type = "l", col = "black", lwd = 1.5, xlim = c(2022.2, 2027.2),
     ylim = c(280, 360),
     main = "CPI: All Urban Consumers (Index)", xlab = "Year", ylab = "Index Level")
lines(cpi_fcst_ts, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
lines(cpi_lower_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)
lines(cpi_upper_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)


# ==============================================================================
# VARIABLE 4: S.P.500 (Log-Differenced)
# ==============================================================================
# 1. Clean historical data
clean_sp <- na.omit(as.numeric(fred_data$S.P.500))
history_sp_ts <- ts(clean_sp, start = c(1959, 1), frequency = 12)
last_sp <- tail(clean_sp, 1)

# 2. Invert log-difference using exponential cumulative product
actual_sp_fcst  <- last_sp * cumprod(exp(var_forecast$fcst$S.P.500[, "fcst"]))
actual_sp_lower <- last_sp * cumprod(exp(var_forecast$fcst$S.P.500[, "lower"]))
actual_sp_upper <- last_sp * cumprod(exp(var_forecast$fcst$S.P.500[, "upper"]))

# 3. Synchronize time indices
sp_end <- time(history_sp_ts)[length(history_sp_ts)]
sp_fcst_ts  <- ts(c(last_sp, actual_sp_fcst),  start = sp_end, frequency = 12)
sp_lower_ts <- ts(c(last_sp, actual_sp_lower), start = sp_end, frequency = 12)
sp_upper_ts <- ts(c(last_sp, actual_sp_upper), start = sp_end, frequency = 12)

# 4. Plot S&P 500 Index
plot(history_sp_ts, type = "l", col = "black", lwd = 1.5, xlim = c(2022.2, 2027.2),
     ylim = c(4000, 7700),
     main = "S&P 500 Index Level", xlab = "Year", ylab = "Index Value")
lines(sp_fcst_ts, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
lines(sp_lower_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)
lines(sp_upper_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)
```
